name: Chocolate-Dipped Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, stir together the sugar and melted butter.
2. Add the Pernod, bourbon, and anise seed to the mixture.
3. Beat in the eggs, then stir in the nuts.
4. Sift the dry ingredients (flour and baking powder) together.
5. Gently stir the dry ingredients into the wet mixture until well incorporated.
6. Cover with plastic wrap and chill for about three hours.
7. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
8. Butter two cookie sheets.
9. Shape the dough into three loaves, each about two inches wide and one-half inch thick, on the prepared cookie sheets.
10. Bake for about 20 minutes, or until the loaves puff up and brown slightly.
11. When cool enough to handle, cut each loaf into diagonal slices about one-half inch thick.
12. Lay the slices on their cut sides and toast them at 375°F for an additional 15 minutes, or until lightly browned.
13. Cool completely.
14. Melt chocolate chips with shortening in a double boiler over low heat, stirring frequently.
15. Remove from heat and stir until the mixture reaches 85°F on a candy thermometer (optional: add chocolate wax for a glossy finish).
16. Dip each biscotti into the melted chocolate, allowing to dry on wax paper with the uncoated side down before placing back in the oven to set.
---The Cereal Murders
Diane Mott Davidson
